title: StatusReport
description: Output of the `status` command: the axis (b) publication-status report (D16). Verdicts are exactly current, corrected, retracted, expression-of-concern, derived from Crossref update-to metadata cross-checked against the OpenAlex is_retracted flag. Axis (b) is independent of axis (a): a reference can be perfectly 'verified' on identity and still be 'retracted' here, and the two axes are reported side by side rather than folded into one verdict. Per-source outcomes reuse the axis (a) vocabulary (confirmed, negative, source_error) so a status that could not be checked is visibly unchecked instead of silently passing as 'current'.

protocol_version.description: Version of the axis (b) decision rules in force for this report.

id.description: Identifier the status was checked for: a lowercased DOI, or a BibTeX key when the entry came from a bib file and carried no DOI.

key.description: BibTeX citation key when the input was a bib file.

checked.description: True only when at least one status source returned a clean answer. When every source returned source_error this is false, and the verdict MUST NOT be presented as a clean 'current': an unchecked status is an absence of evidence, not evidence of currency.

reason.description: Human-readable justification naming which notice or flag drove the verdict.

conflict.description: True when the status sources disagreed (for example OpenAlex reports is_retracted while Crossref carries no retraction update-to). The conflict is reported, never silently resolved away.

notices.description: Every editorial notice found, across sources. An empty array with checked true is what justifies 'current'.

sources.description: One outcome per queried status source. Mandatory: the confirmed / negative / source_error split is what distinguishes 'no notices found' from 'could not ask'.

notice.description: An editorial notice attached to the work by a source.

doi.description: DOI of the notice document itself (the retraction notice, the correction), not of the work it applies to.

label.description: Source-supplied label, for example the Crossref update-to label.

date.description: Notice date, RFC 3339 date or date-time, as reported by the source.

outcome.description: 'confirmed': the source answered and reported at least one notice or flag. 'negative': the source answered cleanly and reported none. 'source_error': the source could not be consulted, so nothing may be inferred from its silence.

is_retracted.description: Raw OpenAlex is_retracted flag, when the source supplies one.

error.description: Required when outcome is 'source_error', forbidden otherwise.

unchecked.description: Entries where every status source errored, so the verdict rests on no clean answer.

statusVerdict.description: Axis (b) verdict, exactly four states. 'corrected' covers corrections, errata, and addenda; 'retracted' covers retractions, withdrawals, and removals; 'expression-of-concern' is reported separately because it is a weaker signal than retraction and must not be collapsed into it.

sourceName.description: Connector name. Status sources are normally crossref and openalex.
